title: Stakna Monastery
tagline: Stakna Monastery — spiritual town of Ladakh (UT)

overview.short: Stakna Monastery or Stakna Gompa is a Buddhist monastery of the Drugpa sect in Stakna, Leh district, Ladakh, northern India, 21 or 25 kilometres from Leh on the left bank of the In
overview.description: Stakna Monastery or Stakna Gompa is a Buddhist monastery of the Drugpa sect in Stakna, Leh district, Ladakh, northern India, 21 or 25 kilometres from Leh on the left bank of the Indus River. It was founded in the late 16th century by a Bhutanese scholar and saint, Chosje Modzin. The name, literally meaning 'tiger's nose' was given because it was built on a hill shaped like a tiger's nose. Of note is a sacred Arya Avalokitesvara statue from Kamrup, Assam.

topPlaces.name: Stakna
topPlaces.description: Stakna is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India, on the banks of the Indus River. The Stakna Monastery is located there.

topPlaces.name: Matho Monastery
topPlaces.description: Matho Monastery, or Matho Gonpa or Mangtro Monastery or Mangtro Gonpa, from the Tibetan "mang" that means "many" and "tro" that means "happiness", is a Tibetan Buddhist monastery located 26 kilometres southeast of Leh in Ladakh, northern India, on the banks of the Indus River. The village of Matho is located at the mouth of a deep gorge running out of the Zanskar Range and across the Indus.

topPlaces.name: Chuchot Gongma
topPlaces.description: Chuchot Gongma is a village-group and the headquarter of Chuchot block in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Thiksey
topPlaces.description: Thiksey is a village and the headquarters of its eponymous block in Leh district, Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Thikse Monastery
topPlaces.description: Thiksey Monastery or Thiksey Gompa (also transliterated from Ladakhi as Thikse, Thiksay or Tikse) is a Buddhist monastery affiliated with the Gelug school of Tibetan Buddhism. It is located on top of a hill in Thiksey approximately 19 kilometres (12 mi) east of Leh, in the Ladakh region of northern India.

topPlaces.name: Nang, Leh
topPlaces.description: Nang is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Changa, Ladakh
topPlaces.description: Changa is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Kharu tehsil, on the bank of the Indus River.

topPlaces.name: Shey
topPlaces.description: Shey is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil, 15 km from Leh towards Hemis.

The village of Matho is located at the mouth of a deep gorge running out of the Zanskar Range and across the Indus.
Chuchot Gongma is a village-group and the headquarter of Chuchot block in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.
Thiksey is a village and the headquarters of its eponymous block in Leh district, Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

Thiksey Monastery or Thiksey Gompa (also transliterated from Ladakhi as Thikse, Thiksay or Tikse) is a Buddhist monastery affiliated with the Gelug school of Tibetan Buddhism. It is located on top of a hill in Thiksey approximately 19 kilometres (12 mi) east of Leh, in the Ladakh region of northern India.
Nang is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.
Changa is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Kharu tehsil, on the bank of the Indus River.
Shey is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil, 15 km from Leh towards Hemis.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Stakna Monastery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Stakna Monastery is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Stakna Monastery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport, Leh (19 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (278 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Stakna Monastery?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Stakna Monastery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Stakna Monastery?
faq.a: Stays in Stakna Monastery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Stakna Monastery?
faq.a: The top attractions are Stakna, Matho Monastery, Chuchot Gongma, Thiksey, Thikse Monastery.
